description: Tenant context is stored in a global variable (globalForTenant.__tenantContext). In Node.js with concurrent requests, if two requests interleave (e.g., async operations yielding to event loop), one request could read the tenant context set by another. The comment acknowledges this ('Next.js Edge Runtime doesn't support async_hooks') but this is a race condition risk in Node.js runtime.
fix: Use AsyncLocalStorage (available in Node.js 16+) for the Node.js runtime. Keep the global fallback only for Edge Runtime. Import from 'node:async_hooks' and wrap each request handler.

title: findUnique tenant check is post-query, leaks data timing
description: The $extends middleware for findUnique does the query first, then checks tenantId on the result. This means: (1) the full row is fetched from DB even if it belongs to another tenant, and (2) timing side-channel could reveal existence of records in other tenants.
fix: For findUnique, convert to findFirst with tenantId in the WHERE clause when tenant context is active.

title: MODELS_WITHOUT_TENANT only lists 2 models, should include more
description: MODELS_WITHOUT_TENANT only contains 'Tenant' and 'VerificationToken'. Other platform-level models like Currency, Permission, PermissionGroup, ExchangeRate should also be excluded. If these models don't have tenantId data, the middleware will try to filter by a non-existent field.
fix: Audit all models and add those that are truly platform-level to MODELS_WITHOUT_TENANT.

title: FormatTypeOption.value @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: FormatTypeOption.value has global @unique. Two tenants cannot create the same format type option value.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, value]).

title: StockTransfer.transferNumber @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: StockTransfer.transferNumber has global @unique. Two tenants cannot both have transfer 'TRF-001'.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, transferNumber]).

title: CrmSnippet shortcut @@unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: CrmSnippet has @@unique([shortcut]) which is global. Two tenants cannot use the same snippet shortcut.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, shortcut]).

title: VoIP audit cleanup $executeRaw bypasses tenant isolation
description: prisma.$executeRaw for log cleanup (line 142) deletes records without tenantId filter, potentially affecting all tenants.
fix: Add tenantId filter or run per-tenant.

title: ProductView has no onDelete for userId and productId FKs
description: ProductView references User and Product without onDelete specification. Should cascade on Product delete, SetNull on User delete.
fix: Add appropriate onDelete behaviors.

title: ProductQuestion has no onDelete specification
description: ProductQuestion references User and Product without onDelete. Should Restrict on User (preserve questions), Cascade on Product (questions are part of product).
fix: Add onDelete: Restrict for userId, onDelete: Cascade for productId.

title: Review has no onDelete for userId FK
description: Review references User without onDelete. Reviews are valuable content and should be preserved with onDelete: Restrict.
fix: Add onDelete: Restrict.

title: Shipping has no onDelete for orderId FK
description: Shipping references Order without onDelete. Shipping records should cascade with orders or be restricted.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: ShippingStatusHistory has no onDelete for shippingId FK
description: ShippingStatusHistory references Shipping without onDelete. Should cascade.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: ForumPost soft-delete inconsistency: has deletedAt but no isDeleted
description: ForumPost and ForumReply have deletedAt fields and indexes, but many queries likely check deletedAt IS NULL. Without a standardized soft-delete pattern (isDeleted Boolean or consistent deletedAt check), queries may return soft-deleted records.
fix: Add isDeleted Boolean @default(false) computed field or ensure all findMany calls include deletedAt: null filter.

title: PromoCodeUsage has no onDelete for promoCodeId FK
description: PromoCodeUsage references PromoCode without onDelete. Usage records should be preserved (Restrict) or cascade.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade (usages are meaningless without their promo code).

title: Conversation has no onDelete for userId/assignedToId FKs
description: Conversation model references User (userId and assignedToId) without onDelete specification.
fix: Add onDelete: SetNull for assignedToId, onDelete: Restrict for userId.

title: Message has no onDelete for conversationId and userId FKs
description: Message references Conversation and User without onDelete. Messages should cascade with conversation.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade for conversationId, onDelete: Restrict for userId.

title: Tenant model has no relation to User for ownership
description: Tenant has ownerUserId String? but no @relation to User. This means Prisma won't enforce the FK constraint and won't support include: { owner: true } queries.
fix: Add owner User? @relation(fields: [ownerUserId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull) and the corresponding reverse relation on User.

title: Media.uploadedBy has no FK relation to User
description: Media model has uploadedBy String? with a TODO comment (F14) acknowledging the missing FK. Without a relation, there is no referential integrity and no cascading behavior.
fix: Add the FK relation as described in the TODO: uploader User? @relation('MediaUploader', fields: [uploadedBy], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict).

title: AuditTrail.userId has no FK relation to User
description: AuditTrail has userId String but no @relation to User. Audit trail records should be preserved even if user is deleted, but the FK would add referential integrity.
fix: Add user User? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull) or keep as String but document the design decision.

title: AuditLog.userId has no FK relation to User
description: AuditLog has userId String? but no @relation to User. Similar to AuditTrail, the FK is missing.
fix: Add user User? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull).

title: CustomFieldValue has no FK relation for entityId
description: CustomFieldValue uses entityType/entityId polymorphic pattern without FK. Deleting the entity (Product, Order, etc.) leaves orphaned custom field values.
fix: Consider separate CustomFieldValue tables per entity type, or implement cleanup logic.

title: DocumentAttachment uses polymorphic FK without referential integrity
description: DocumentAttachment uses entityType/entityId polymorphic pattern. Deleting the referenced entity leaves orphaned attachments with no cleanup path.
fix: Implement cleanup triggers or cron job, or use separate attachment tables per entity type.

title: ContentInteraction uses polymorphic FK without referential integrity
description: ContentInteraction has contentId/contentType pattern referencing Video, SocialPost, or Media. No FK enforcement, orphaned records possible.
fix: Implement cleanup logic or use separate interaction tables.

title: PriceWatch has no onDelete for userId and productId FKs
description: PriceWatch references User and Product without onDelete. Should cascade on product delete, set null or restrict on user delete.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade for productId, onDelete: SetNull for userId.

title: Discount model has no onDelete for categoryId FK
description: Discount references Category via categoryId without onDelete behavior specified.
fix: Add onDelete: SetNull (keep discount but remove category restriction) or onDelete: Cascade.

title: Subscription has no onDelete for userId FK
description: Subscription references User without onDelete specification.
fix: Add onDelete: Restrict (cannot delete user with active subscriptions).

title: CrmLead has no onDelete for assignedToId FK
description: CrmLead references User (assignedToId) with onDelete: SetNull which is appropriate, but the lead itself has no soft-delete pattern for data retention compliance.
fix: Add deletedAt DateTime? field and @@index([deletedAt]) for GDPR data retention.

title: EmailLog has no FK relations for userId or templateId
description: EmailLog has userId and templateId as plain String fields without @relation. No referential integrity.
fix: Add proper FK relations with onDelete: SetNull.

title: BudgetLine has no onDelete for budgetId or accountId FKs
description: BudgetLine references Budget and ChartOfAccount without onDelete. Lines should cascade with their budget.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade for budgetId, onDelete: Restrict for accountId.

title: FixedAssetDepreciation has no onDelete for assetId FK
description: FixedAssetDepreciation references FixedAsset without onDelete. Depreciation records should cascade with the asset.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: DialerListEntry has no onDelete for campaignId FK
description: DialerListEntry references DialerCampaign without onDelete. Entries should cascade with their campaign.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: DialerDisposition has no onDelete for callLogId FK
description: DialerDisposition references CallLog without onDelete.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: Discount model missing composite index for active discount queries
description: Discount has isActive, startDate, endDate fields commonly queried together for 'currently valid discounts' but no composite index.
fix: Add @@index([isActive, startDate, endDate]) for efficient active discount lookups.

title: AbandonedCart missing composite index for recovery cron queries
description: AbandonedCart is queried by (recoveredAt IS NULL AND createdAt > X) for the abandonment recovery cron, but lacks a composite index.
fix: Add @@index([recoveredAt, createdAt]) for the recovery cron query pattern.

title: EmailLog missing composite index for campaign analytics queries
description: EmailLog is queried by (sentAt, status) for analytics but has no composite index on these fields.
fix: Add @@index([sentAt, status]) and @@index([templateId, sentAt]).

title: CallLog missing composite index for dashboard trend queries
description: The VoIP dashboard raw query groups by DATE(startedAt) and filters by startedAt range and status. No composite index on (startedAt, status).
fix: Add @@index([startedAt, status]) for the dashboard trends query.

title: DEFAULT_FIND_MANY_LIMIT of 200 may be too high for large tables
description: The default findMany limit is 200 records. For tables like SearchLog, AuditLog, EmailLog that can have millions of rows, returning 200 records by default may still cause performance issues, especially with large includes.
fix: Consider lowering the default to 50-100, or implementing per-model defaults based on average row size.

title: findMany for recent emails without select, over-fetching
description: prisma.emailLog.findMany with take: 20 but includes all columns by default. The select on line 144 limits to some fields but could be more restrictive.
fix: The select is partially implemented. Good practice but ensure no large text fields are included unnecessarily.

title: Product model has 20+ indexes, potential write performance impact
description: The Product model has over 20 indexes. While this is good for reads, every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE must update all 20 indexes. For a high-write table like Product (frequent stock updates), this can cause write performance degradation.
fix: Audit which indexes are actually used in queries. Remove unused indexes. Consider moving frequently-updated fields (stockQuantity, views) to a separate table.

title: Order model has 17 indexes including redundant ones
description: Order model has @@index([orderNumber]) but orderNumber is already @unique (which creates an implicit index). The explicit @@index is redundant. Same for any field with @unique that also has @@index.
fix: Remove @@index([orderNumber]) as it is redundant with the @unique constraint. Audit other models for the same pattern.

title: BlogPost.slug has both @unique and @@index, redundant index
description: BlogPost has slug @unique AND @@index([slug]). The @unique constraint already creates a unique index. The explicit @@index is wasteful. Same pattern on: HeroSlide, NewsArticle, Page, Guide, Webinar, ForumCategory.
fix: Remove the redundant @@index([slug]) on all affected content models.

title: WebhookEvent.eventId has both @unique and @@index, redundant
description: WebhookEvent has eventId @unique AND @@index([eventId]). Redundant index wastes storage and slows writes.
fix: Remove @@index([eventId]).

title: CustomerMetrics missing index for loyalty tier queries
description: CustomerMetrics has totalSpent, orderCount, avgOrderValue used for loyalty tier calculations but no composite index.
fix: Add @@index([userId, totalSpent]) for tier calculation queries.

title: SmsLog missing composite index for common query patterns
description: SmsLog has userId, status, sentAt fields commonly queried together for SMS history but lacks a composite index.
fix: Add @@index([userId, sentAt]) and @@index([status, sentAt]).

title: LoyaltyTransaction missing composite index for expiration queries
description: LoyaltyTransaction has expiresAt and type fields. The points-expiring cron queries by (expiresAt <= now AND type IN [EARN_*]) but has no composite index.
fix: Add @@index([expiresAt, type]) for the expiration cron query pattern.

title: InventoryReservation missing composite index for expiration cleanup
description: InventoryReservation has status and expiresAt fields. The reservation cleanup job queries by (status = RESERVED AND expiresAt < now) but @@index([expiresAt]) alone is not optimal.
fix: Add @@index([status, expiresAt]) composite index.

title: VoIP audit log queries have no result size limit
description: Multiple $queryRaw calls in audit-log.ts aggregate data without LIMIT clauses.
fix: Add appropriate LIMIT clauses to prevent excessive data transfer.

title: Tags raw query returns all distinct tags without limit
description: prisma.$queryRaw for admin tags returns all unique tags without a LIMIT clause. As tags grow, this becomes increasingly expensive.
fix: Add LIMIT 1000 or implement pagination.

title: Search results raw query could return large payloads
description: The main search query in search.ts returns full product data (including description text) for matching products. For broad searches, this could be a significant payload.
fix: Use a select list in the raw SQL to only return fields needed for search results display (id, name, slug, price, imageUrl).

title: JournalEntry missing composite index for accounting period queries
description: JournalEntry is frequently queried by (type, status, date range) for period close and reporting, but lacks the composite index @@index([type, status, date]).
fix: The model already has @@index([type, deletedAt]) and @@index([status, deletedAt]) but add @@index([type, status, date]) for period-based queries.

title: BankTransaction missing composite index for reconciliation queries
description: BankTransaction is queried by (bankAccountId, isReconciled, date) for bank reconciliation but lacks the composite index.
fix: Add @@index([bankAccountId, isReconciled, date]) for reconciliation query optimization.

title: PriceBook and PriceBookEntry missing composite indexes
description: PriceBookEntry needs a composite index on (priceBookId, productId) for efficient price lookups by product within a price book.
fix: Add @@index([priceBookId, productId]) to PriceBookEntry.

title: CrmTask missing composite index for agent task list queries
description: CrmTask is queried by (assignedToId, status) for the agent task list but lacks this composite index.
fix: Add @@index([assignedToId, status, dueDate]) for agent task queries.

title: EmailFlowExecution missing composite index for flow processing
description: EmailFlowExecution is queried by (flowId, status) during automation processing but lacks this composite index.
fix: Add @@index([flowId, status]).

title: Batch accounting operations do sequential $queryRaw for each entry
description: batch-operations.service.ts calls $queryRaw for MAX journal number multiple times (lines 139, 160) within batch processing. Each creates a separate DB round-trip.
fix: Pre-allocate a range of journal numbers with a single query at the start of the batch, then assign them sequentially.

title: Recurring entries generate journal numbers one at a time
description: recurring-entries.service.ts generates each recurring journal entry with a separate MAX query (line 321). For monthly processing of 50+ recurring entries, this is 50+ sequential queries.
fix: Use a sequence or pre-allocate numbers for the batch.

title: Product.sku has @@index but should be @@unique([tenantId, sku]) for dedup
description: Product.sku has only @@index but no uniqueness constraint. Two products within the same tenant could have the same SKU, causing inventory/order confusion.
fix: Add @@unique([tenantId, sku]) constraint (nullable SKUs should be excluded via a filtered index or app-level check).

title: ProductOption.sku has no unique constraint
description: ProductOption has sku field but no uniqueness constraint. Different options could share the same SKU within a tenant.
fix: Add @@unique([tenantId, sku]) or at minimum @@unique([productId, sku]).

title: EmailCampaign.stats stored as Json without proper indexing
description: EmailCampaign stores aggregate stats (opened, clicked, etc.) in a Json field that is updated via raw SQL jsonb_set operations. This pattern is fragile and not queryable via Prisma.
fix: Consider moving stats to dedicated columns (openCount Int, clickCount Int, etc.) with proper indexes for analytics queries.

title: CrmTicket missing composite index for agent queue queries
description: CrmTicket is queried by (assigneeId, status, priority) for the agent ticket queue. Without a composite index, the queue page will be slow.
fix: Add @@index([assigneeId, status, priority]).

title: SearchLog grows unbounded without TTL or partition strategy
description: SearchLog stores every search query indefinitely. At scale (thousands of searches/day), this table will grow to millions of rows without any cleanup strategy. The raw analytics queries will become increasingly slow.
fix: Add a retention policy: cron job to delete records older than 90 days, or partition the table by month.

title: PerformanceLog grows unbounded without TTL
description: PerformanceLog records every API call's performance. Without cleanup, this table will grow to millions of rows quickly.
fix: Add retention policy: delete records older than 30 days, or use time-series partitioning.

title: AuditLog grows unbounded without TTL or archival
description: AuditLog stores every audit event indefinitely. For compliance, keep recent data in hot storage and archive older records.
fix: Implement archival strategy: move records older than 1 year to a separate archive table or cold storage.

title: ContentInteraction grows unbounded without TTL
description: ContentInteraction records every view, click, share. This is a high-volume analytics table that will grow very fast without cleanup.
fix: Add retention policy or aggregate old records into daily/weekly summaries.

title: CrmActivity grows unbounded and has no archival strategy
description: CrmActivity records every sales activity (call, email, meeting, note). Over months, this table can grow very large, impacting CRM timeline queries.
fix: Implement archival for activities older than 2 years, or add @@index([performedById, createdAt]) for efficient timeline pagination.

title: ExchangeRate missing composite index for currency pair + date lookups
description: ExchangeRate is queried by (fromCurrency, toCurrency, date) for exchange rate lookups but may lack the optimal composite index.
fix: Add @@unique([fromCurrency, toCurrency, date]) or @@index([fromCurrency, toCurrency, date]).

title: Company.slug @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: Company.slug has global @unique. Two tenants cannot have a company with the same slug.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, slug]).

title: Company.ownerId @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: Company.ownerId has global @unique, meaning one user can only own one company across ALL tenants. If a user is an owner in Tenant A, they cannot be an owner in Tenant B.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, ownerId]).

title: NotificationPreference.userId @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: NotificationPreference has userId @unique globally. A user can only have one notification preference across all tenants. Also Cart.userId @unique (ecommerce line 39) has the same issue.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, userId]).

title: User.stripeCustomerId @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: User.stripeCustomerId has global @unique. This is likely correct if using a single Stripe account, but in multi-tenant with separate Stripe accounts per tenant, the same customer ID from different Stripe accounts could collide.
fix: If tenants have separate Stripe accounts, replace with @@unique([tenantId, stripeCustomerId]).

title: UpsellConfig has no FK relation for productId and upsellProductId
description: UpsellConfig references products by ID strings without FK relations. Deleting a product leaves orphaned upsell configs.
fix: Add proper @relation directives with onDelete: Cascade.

title: RSDeProject and RSDeExpense have no onDelete cascade
description: RSDeExpense references RSDeProject without onDelete. Expenses should cascade with their project.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade on the project relation.

title: RSDeCalculation has no onDelete for projectId FK
description: RSDeCalculation references RSDeProject without onDelete. Calculations should cascade with their project.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: InboundEmailAttachment has no onDelete for inboundEmailId FK
description: InboundEmailAttachment references InboundEmail without onDelete. Attachments should cascade.
fix: Add onDelete: Cascade.

title: ChartOfAccount.code @unique is global, not tenant-scoped
description: ChartOfAccount.code has global @unique. Two tenants cannot both have account code '1000' (Cash). This completely breaks multi-tenant accounting.
fix: Replace with @@unique([tenantId, code]).

title: ProspectList missing index for status-based queries
description: ProspectList is queried by status (DRAFT, ACTIVE, etc.) but may lack an efficient index for the common admin listing query.
fix: Add @@index([status, createdAt]) for listing and filtering.

title: 753 routes (82%) lack CSRF protection
description: Only 166 out of 919 routes implement CSRF token validation. While routes using withAdminGuard get CSRF protection automatically for mutation methods, many non-admin routes that accept POST/PUT/PATCH requests do not validate CSRF tokens. This includes auth routes (login, register, signup), account routes, cart routes, community routes, and public-facing routes. Note: withAdminGuard handles CSRF for its 615 wrapped routes, so the effective unprotected count for admin mutation routes is low, but non-admin mutation routes remain exposed.
fix: For non-admin mutation routes, implement CSRF validation using the existing validateCsrf() middleware from @/lib/csrf-middleware. At minimum, protect: auth routes (login, register, signup, forgot-password, reset-password), account mutation routes, cart/checkout routes, and community post routes.

title: 736 routes (80%) lack explicit rate limiting
description: Only 183 out of 919 routes implement rate limiting. While withAdminGuard provides rate limiting for 615 admin routes (Redis-backed with in-memory fallback), many public-facing and account routes lack rate limiting. Auth routes (login, register, forgot-password, signup, reset-password) DO have rate limiting, which is good. But account endpoints, cart endpoints, product endpoints, community endpoints, and public API endpoints do not. Sample unprotected high-value routes: /api/account/password, /api/account/profile, /api/cart/*, /api/checkout/*, /api/community/posts/*.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware to all public-facing mutation endpoints. Priority: checkout routes, account mutation routes, contact/demo-request forms, public chatbot routes. Use the existing rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter.

title: 395 routes (43%) lack Zod input validation
description: 395 out of 919 routes do not use Zod schemas for input validation. 85 of these are mutation routes (POST/PUT/PATCH) that accept user input without schema validation. Breakdown by area: admin (199 routes), accounting (54), cron (29), account (16), v1 API (8), products (7), platform (7), voip (6), auth (6). Many GET-only routes legitimately don't need Zod (they only read query params), but all mutation routes should validate their bodies.
fix: Add Zod schemas to all 85 mutation routes that lack them. Priority: platform routes (tenant creation, domain setup, billing), accounting mutation routes (journal entries, invoices), auth routes (login, oauth), and any route that writes to the database.

title: 77 routes lack try/catch error handling
description: 77 out of 919 routes lack try/catch blocks. Routes wrapped in withAdminGuard get error handling from the guard itself, but routes without guards that also lack try/catch will crash with unhandled exceptions, potentially exposing stack traces in development or returning generic 500 errors. Sample routes without error handling: src/app/api/v1/customers/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/customers/[id]/notes/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/products/[id]/sales/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/crm/qa-forms/route.ts.
fix: Add try/catch blocks to all 77 routes. For routes using withAdminGuard, the guard's outer try/catch provides coverage, so focus on routes that don't use any guard wrapper.

title: Public chat route accessible without auth, rate limiting, or CSRF
description: The /api/public/chat and /api/public/chatbot endpoints are completely unprotected - no authentication, no rate limiting, no CSRF. These endpoints likely accept user input (chat messages) and could be abused for spam, denial of service, or injection attacks.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP), input length validation, and basic abuse detection. Consider adding a simple CAPTCHA or proof-of-work challenge for anonymous users.

title: Demo request form has no auth, rate limiting, or CSRF protection
description: The /api/demo-request endpoint is a public form submission endpoint with no protections. It can be abused for: spam (submitting thousands of fake demo requests), email bombing (if it sends confirmation emails), and resource exhaustion.
fix: Add rate limiting (5 requests/minute per IP), Zod validation for input, and consider adding a honeypot field or reCAPTCHA integration.

title: Click tracking endpoint has no auth or rate limiting
description: The /api/tracking/click and /api/tracking/email endpoints are publicly accessible without any protections. An attacker could: inflate click analytics, forge email open tracking, manipulate campaign metrics, or cause excessive database writes.
fix: Add rate limiting per IP. Validate that the tracking token/ID is a valid format. Consider using signed URLs with HMAC to prevent fabricated tracking events.

title: Mailing list confirmation has no rate limiting
description: The /api/mailing-list/confirm endpoint is publicly accessible without rate limiting. An attacker could brute-force confirmation tokens or cause excessive database lookups.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP). Ensure confirmation tokens are sufficiently random (at least 32 bytes) and have an expiry.

title: Consent token endpoint has no rate limiting or token format validation
description: The /api/consent/[token] endpoint processes consent tokens without rate limiting. Token-based endpoints are vulnerable to brute-force enumeration if tokens are short or predictable.
fix: Add rate limiting. Validate token format before database lookup. Ensure tokens are cryptographically random and at least 32 bytes.

title: Estimates token endpoint accessible without auth or rate limiting
description: The /api/estimates/[token] endpoint allows access to estimates/quotes via a token URL. Without rate limiting, an attacker could enumerate tokens to access business estimates containing pricing and client information.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP). Ensure tokens are cryptographically secure (UUID v4 minimum). Add token expiry if not already present.

title: 251 POST routes return 200 instead of 201 for resource creation
description: Out of 466 POST handlers, only 215 return status 201 for resource creation. The remaining 251 return 200, which violates HTTP semantics. While not a security vulnerability per se, incorrect status codes can mask issues in monitoring, cause confusion in API consumers, and make audit logging less precise.
fix: Review all POST handlers that create resources and change their success status to 201. POST handlers that perform actions (not creation) can keep 200.

title: Webhook routes (13) have inconsistent signature verification patterns
description: The 13 webhook routes use varied authentication patterns: HMAC-SHA256 with timing-safe comparison (stripe/paypal/meta/teams/zapier/shipping/email-bounce/email-inbound/sms-inbound - good), Webex signature verification via library (webex - good), Twilio signature when header present (whatsapp - conditional), no verification (voip/webhooks/sms - bad), and gone/disabled (stripe duplicate - fine). The inconsistency means some webhooks could accept forged requests.
fix: Ensure ALL webhook routes verify the sender's signature. Specifically fix /api/voip/webhooks/sms to verify Telnyx webhook signatures. Make the WhatsApp webhook's Twilio signature check mandatory (not conditional on header presence).

title: Cron routes use inconsistent auth patterns (some via CRON_SECRET, some via withAdminGuard)
description: Cron routes under /api/cron/ and /api/admin/*/cron/ use different auth strategies. Some accept CRON_SECRET Bearer token OR admin session (good dual auth), while others only check session. If CRON_SECRET is not set, some cron routes fall back to requiring admin session only, which means automated cron execution fails silently. 29 cron routes need standardized auth.
fix: Create a withCronGuard middleware that standardizes: CRON_SECRET validation (timing-safe), fallback to admin session, proper logging, and rate limiting. Apply to all 29 cron routes.

title: Search and product endpoints lack rate limiting (DoS risk)
description: The /api/search/suggest, /api/search/popular, /api/products/compare, /api/products/recommendations, and /api/products/[id]/related routes are public, have no authentication, and no rate limiting. These endpoints likely execute database queries that could be expensive. An attacker could flood these endpoints to cause database overload or increase cloud costs.
fix: Add rate limiting to all search and product listing endpoints. Suggested limits: 30 requests/minute per IP for search, 60 requests/minute per IP for product reads. Use the existing rateLimitMiddleware.

title: Chat conversation update uses spread operator on validated data (potential mass assignment)
description: The DELETE handler at line 263 uses prisma.conversation.delete (hard delete). The PUT handler at line 208 uses `const updateData: Record<string, unknown> = { ...data }` which spreads potentially untrusted data into the update operation.
fix: Replace hard delete with soft delete. In the PUT handler, explicitly whitelist the fields that can be updated rather than spreading the data object.

title: Client portal token routes (4) lack rate limiting for token enumeration protection
description: The 4 accounting client-portal routes (/api/accounting/client-portal/[token]/invoices, /payments, /estimates, /statement) use token-based access (validatePortalAccess). While tokens provide auth, there is no rate limiting to prevent brute-force token enumeration. If tokens are short or sequential, attackers could access other clients' financial data.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP). Ensure portal tokens are UUIDv4 or longer (128+ bits of entropy). Add token expiry and revocation. Log failed token lookups for monitoring.

title: Auth routes lack CSRF protection on mutation endpoints
description: Auth routes (/api/auth/login, /api/auth/register, /api/auth/signup, /api/auth/forgot-password, /api/auth/reset-password, /api/auth/accept-invite) accept POST requests without CSRF token validation. While these routes do have rate limiting (which mitigates brute force), the lack of CSRF means an attacker could forge requests on behalf of a user who visits a malicious page (login CSRF, forced registration, unwanted password reset emails).
fix: Add CSRF validation to all auth mutation endpoints using validateCsrf() from @/lib/csrf-middleware. Login CSRF is particularly dangerous as it can force a victim to log into the attacker's account.

title: CRM quotas route uses spread operator on data without full validation
description: Line 25 shows `...data` being spread into a Prisma operation. If the data object contains fields not in the Zod schema (or if .passthrough() is used), extra fields could be injected into the database.
fix: Destructure only expected fields from validated data. Never spread validated data directly if the Zod schema might allow extra fields.

title: No tests for Zod schema completeness on critical mutation routes
description: While many routes have Zod schemas, there are no tests verifying that: (1) schemas reject unexpected fields (no .passthrough()), (2) schemas enforce proper types and lengths, (3) schemas handle edge cases (empty strings, null, undefined, very long inputs). Critical schemas to test: createCustomerSchema, updateCustomerSchema, mergeSchema, tenant creation, order creation, invoice creation.
fix: Create Zod schema tests for each critical model. Test with: valid input, missing required fields, extra fields (should be stripped), boundary values (max length strings, negative numbers), and injection attempts (SQL in strings, HTML in text fields).

title: No regression tests for previously fixed security vulnerabilities (FAILLE-*)
description: The codebase references multiple fixed security vulnerabilities: FAILLE-002 (permission bypass), FAILLE-009 (rate limiting), FAILLE-022 (error message exposure), FAILLE-055 (IP spoofing), FAILLE-066 (body size), FAILLE-078 (rate limit bucket). There are no automated tests ensuring these fixes remain in place during future refactoring.
fix: Create security-regression.test.ts with test cases for each FAILLE-* fix: (1) FAILLE-002: non-permitted user gets 403, (2) FAILLE-009: rate limiter uses Redis, (3) FAILLE-022: 500 errors don't expose messages, (4) FAILLE-055: IP extraction uses rightmost XFF, (5) FAILLE-066: >1MB body returns 413, (6) FAILLE-078: dynamic path segments are normalized.

title: No tests verifying DELETE routes properly check resource ownership
description: 130 DELETE handlers exist across the codebase. While most are behind withAdminGuard, there are no tests verifying that: (1) users can only delete their own resources, (2) cross-tenant deletion is blocked, (3) cascading deletes don't affect other tenants' data. In a multi-tenant system, this is critical.
fix: Create ownership-check tests for DELETE endpoints, especially: account routes (can user A delete user B's wishlist?), and admin routes in multi-tenant context (can tenant A's admin delete tenant B's data?).

title: No security scanning in CI/CD for new routes missing guards
description: The codebase has 919 routes and growing. There is no automated check in the CI/CD pipeline that verifies new routes meet minimum security requirements (auth guard, input validation, error handling). This means developers can accidentally push unprotected routes to production.
fix: Add a CI step that runs a script checking: (1) all admin routes use withAdminGuard, (2) all mutation routes have Zod validation, (3) all routes have try/catch or a guard wrapper, (4) no debug routes exist outside development. The script should fail the build if violations are found.

title: Temporary password uses Math.random() which is not cryptographically secure
description: Line 100: `const tempPassword = \`Koraline-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 10)}\``. Math.random() is not a CSPRNG (Cryptographically Secure Pseudo-Random Number Generator). The generated passwords are predictable if the internal state of the PRNG is known or can be sampled. This is a tenant owner's initial password.
fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex') or crypto.randomUUID() for generating temporary passwords. Example: `const tempPassword = \`Koraline-${crypto.randomBytes(8).toString('hex')}\``;

title: VoIP routes (36) include 2 unprotected endpoints beyond webhooks
description: Out of 36 VoIP routes, 2 have no auth: /api/voip/debug (API key exposure, discussed in C1-SEC-S-051) and /api/voip/webhooks/sms (no signature verification, discussed in C1-SEC-S-063). The remaining 34 routes properly use auth(). However, the /api/voip/credentials route returns sipPassword in plaintext (discussed in C1-SEC-S-054).
fix: Delete /api/voip/debug, add signature verification to /api/voip/webhooks/sms, and stop returning sipPassword from /api/voip/credentials.

title: Platform routes (11) handle critical operations with inconsistent security
description: The 11 platform routes handle tenant management, billing, licensing, domain setup, and health checks. They use a mix of: withAdminGuard (some), custom auth() + header checks (impersonate, assisted-setup, tenants), and no auth (health). The impersonate and assisted-setup routes are the most dangerous and have the weakest auth (header-based). The provision, checkout, and billing-portal routes handle Stripe operations and lack Zod validation.
fix: Migrate all platform management routes to use withAdminGuard with requiredPermission: 'platform.manage'. Add Zod validation to provision, checkout, and billing-portal routes. Fix the header-based auth in impersonate and assisted-setup.

title: Magazine tutorial route has weak path sanitization
description: Line 21 uses `slugPath.replace(/\.\./g, '').replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_\-\/\.]/g, '')` which removes '..' after the fact but allows dots in the final path. The sanitization is applied after joining the slug array, and the replacement of '..' could be bypassed with encodings like '..%2F' in some configurations. While the route does have auth (session check), the sanitization approach is fragile.
fix: Use path.resolve() and verify the resolved path starts with the expected base directory. Better: use a whitelist approach - map slugs to known file paths rather than constructing paths from user input.

title: withAdminGuard completeness review: well-implemented but has header trust issue
description: The admin-api-guard.ts is well-architected with 7 security layers: (1) Authentication via auth(), (2) Body size limits (1MB), (3) Role check (EMPLOYEE/OWNER), (4) Permission check (optional), (5) MFA enforcement (optional), (6) CSRF validation for mutations, (7) Rate limiting (Redis with memory fallback). Issues found: (a) trusts x-tenant-slug and x-tenant-super-admin headers for tenant context (C1-SEC-S-057), (b) ADMIN_ROLES allows EMPLOYEE which may be too broad for some operations, (c) IP extraction regex is generous (/^[\d.:a-fA-F]{3,45}$/).
fix: Fix the header trust issue (C1-SEC-S-057). Consider narrowing ADMIN_ROLES for sensitive operations via requiredPermission. Tighten the IP regex to match only valid IPv4/IPv6 formats.

title: No Zod validation: Tenant provisioning accepts unvalidated body
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Creates tenants and Stripe subscriptions from raw request body without schema validation. Could inject unexpected tenant configuration.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Checkout/billing accepts unvalidated body
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Handles Stripe checkout session creation without validating the plan, modules, or pricing parameters.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Custom domain setup accepts unvalidated body
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Domain verification and DNS setup without validating the domain format, potentially allowing SSRF or DNS rebinding.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: License management accepts unvalidated body
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Per-seat license changes without validating quantity or user IDs, could allow license manipulation.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Billing portal redirect without validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Creates Stripe billing portal session without validating return URL, potential open redirect.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Admin password reset without input validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Allows admins to reset user passwords without validating the new password format or strength via Zod.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Impersonation body not validated
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. tenantId accepted without Zod validation - could receive unexpected types or injection attempts.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Tenant creation body not validated
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. slug, name, plan, and configuration accepted without Zod schema. Could inject invalid slugs or unexpected config.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Inventory import without schema validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Bulk inventory data imported without Zod validation. Import endpoints process external data and need strict schemas.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Media upload without content validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. File upload endpoint without Zod validation on metadata fields (title, description, alt text).
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Transcription request without validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Triggers video transcription without validating parameters like language, format, or options.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No Zod validation: Phone system config without validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Phone system configuration changes without schema validation. Could corrupt VoIP settings.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: Hard delete: EmailTemplate permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.emailTemplate.delete(). Email templates may be referenced by automation flows. Hard delete could break active email campaigns.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: Video permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.video.delete(). Videos may be embedded in products and pages. Hard delete causes broken embeds and missing media.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: Bundle permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.bundle.delete(). Product bundles linked to orders. Hard delete may orphan order line items.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: Review permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.review.delete(). Customer reviews are user-generated content with SEO value. Hard delete removes social proof.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: Discount permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.discount.delete(). Promotions/discounts may be referenced in order history. Hard delete breaks discount audit trail.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: PromoCode permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.promoCode.delete(). Promo codes may have been used in orders. Hard delete breaks usage tracking and fraud detection.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: NewsletterSubscriber permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.newsletterSubscriber.delete(). Subscriber deletion should be soft to maintain suppression list for Loi C-28 compliance.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: Hard delete: DialerCampaign permanently destroyed
description: Route uses prisma.dialerCampaign.delete(). Dialer campaigns contain call log associations. Hard delete may orphan call records.
fix: Replace with soft delete pattern: update deletedAt timestamp instead of destroying the record.

title: No try/catch: V1 public API customers endpoint
description: Route handler lacks try/catch error handling. Public API routes must have robust error handling to prevent leaking internal errors to external consumers. Unhandled exceptions return Next.js default 500 error which may expose internal details.
fix: Wrap the handler logic in try/catch. In catch, log the error with logger.error() and return a generic error response: NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 }).

title: No try/catch: V1 public API products endpoint
description: Route handler lacks try/catch error handling. Unhandled Prisma errors could expose database schema details to API consumers. Unhandled exceptions return Next.js default 500 error which may expose internal details.
fix: Wrap the handler logic in try/catch. In catch, log the error with logger.error() and return a generic error response: NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 }).

title: No try/catch: V1 public API single product endpoint
description: Route handler lacks try/catch error handling. Product lookup errors not caught, could expose Prisma error messages. Unhandled exceptions return Next.js default 500 error which may expose internal details.
fix: Wrap the handler logic in try/catch. In catch, log the error with logger.error() and return a generic error response: NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 }).

title: No try/catch: V1 public API invoices endpoint
description: Route handler lacks try/catch error handling. Financial data endpoint without error handling. Prisma errors could leak invoice schema. Unhandled exceptions return Next.js default 500 error which may expose internal details.
fix: Wrap the handler logic in try/catch. In catch, log the error with logger.error() and return a generic error response: NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 }).

title: No rate limiting: Contact platforms listing
description: Route lacks rate limiting. Reveals configured communication platforms without rate limiting.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter. Suggested limit depends on endpoint type: sensitive operations (5-10/min), read operations (30-60/min).

title: No rate limiting: Saved cart operations
description: Route lacks rate limiting. Cart deletion endpoint could be used for DoS against user carts.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter. Suggested limit depends on endpoint type: sensitive operations (5-10/min), read operations (30-60/min).

title: No rate limiting: Profile update
description: Route lacks rate limiting. Profile updates without rate limiting could be used for enumeration or DoS.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter. Suggested limit depends on endpoint type: sensitive operations (5-10/min), read operations (30-60/min).

title: No rate limiting: Community post operations
description: Route lacks rate limiting. Community post creation/editing without rate limiting enables spam.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter. Suggested limit depends on endpoint type: sensitive operations (5-10/min), read operations (30-60/min).

title: No rate limiting: Review voting
description: Route lacks rate limiting. Vote manipulation possible without rate limiting on the voting endpoint.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter. Suggested limit depends on endpoint type: sensitive operations (5-10/min), read operations (30-60/min).

title: No CSRF: Profile update
description: Mutation endpoint lacks CSRF token validation. CSRF could modify user profile data (name, email, phone).
fix: Add validateCsrf(request) check at the start of mutation handlers (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE). Import from @/lib/csrf-middleware.

title: No CSRF: Cart operations
description: Mutation endpoint lacks CSRF token validation. CSRF could clear user's shopping cart.
fix: Add validateCsrf(request) check at the start of mutation handlers (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE). Import from @/lib/csrf-middleware.

title: No Zod: Journal entry posting without validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod input validation. Posting a journal entry is irreversible. Input should be validated before executing.
fix: Add Zod schema for request body/params validation. Use .safeParse() and return 400 for invalid input.

title: No Zod: Payroll calculation without validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod input validation. Payroll calculations should validate parameters (pay period, deductions, rates).
fix: Add Zod schema for request body/params validation. Use .safeParse() and return 400 for invalid input.

title: No Zod: OCR scan without file validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod input validation. OCR file processing without validating file type, size, or format.
fix: Add Zod schema for request body/params validation. Use .safeParse() and return 400 for invalid input.

title: No Zod: Invoice send without validation
description: POST handler lacks Zod input validation. Sending invoices to customers without validating recipient or message parameters.
fix: Add Zod schema for request body/params validation. Use .safeParse() and return 400 for invalid input.

title: No tests for multi-tenant data isolation in API routes
description: In a multi-tenant system with 919 routes, there are no tests verifying that tenant A's API requests cannot access tenant B's data. The withAdminGuard sets tenant context, but there are no automated tests proving this isolation works correctly across all routes.
fix: Create multi-tenant isolation tests: create two tenants with test data, authenticate as each, and verify cross-tenant data is inaccessible via API calls.

title: No tests for API response time/performance budgets
description: No performance tests exist for API routes. Database-heavy routes (search, accounting reports, CRM analytics) could degrade under load. Without performance baselines, regressions go undetected.
fix: Add performance budget tests for critical routes: product search < 200ms, admin dashboard < 500ms, accounting reports < 2s. Alert on regressions.

title: SIP extension hard delete exposes VoIP infrastructure to misconfiguration
description: Deleting SIP extensions via prisma.sipExtension.delete() immediately invalidates VoIP routing. If an extension is deleted while calls are active, those calls will fail. No grace period or pre-flight check for active calls.
fix: Replace with soft delete. Before deletion, check for active calls on the extension. Implement a grace period before hard cleanup.

title: Phone number hard delete could orphan Telnyx resource
description: Deleting phone number record via prisma.phoneNumber.delete() removes the DB record but may not release the number at Telnyx, creating a billing orphan. Conversely, if the Telnyx API call fails after DB delete, the state is inconsistent.
fix: Implement two-phase delete: first release at Telnyx, then soft-delete the DB record. Use a transaction to ensure consistency.

title: Loyalty tier hard delete could reset customer statuses
description: LoyaltyTierConfig records are permanently deleted. Customers assigned to this tier will have a dangling reference. The loyalty system may malfunction for affected customers.
fix: Soft delete. Before deletion, reassign customers in the deleted tier to a default tier. Prevent deletion if customers are assigned.

title: Email account hard delete breaks email sending capability
description: EmailAccount records (SMTP/API credentials) are permanently deleted. If this is the only configured sending account, the system loses email delivery capability. No fallback check.
fix: Soft delete. Before deletion, verify at least one other active email account exists. Warn if this is the last account.

title: Community seed route is a mutation endpoint without Zod validation
description: The /api/community/seed endpoint is a POST handler that likely creates test/seed data. Without Zod validation, it could accept malicious payloads. Seed endpoints should not exist in production.
fix: Either delete this endpoint or gate it behind NODE_ENV === 'development' check AND withAdminGuard. Add Zod validation for any accepted parameters.

title: 18 customer-facing public pages lack i18n
description: 18 pages under src/app/(public)/ have no i18n integration. These are publicly accessible and customer-facing. Includes: onboarding, signup, solutions (5 pages), docs, support, resources/webinaires, presse, carrieres, privacy, terms, plan-du-site, checkout/[slug], cours/[slug], order tracking. Some may be server wrappers delegating to i18n-aware client components, but most contain hardcoded French text.
fix: Audit each page. For those with hardcoded strings, add i18n support. Priority order: onboarding > signup > solutions > checkout > others.

title: Hardcoded placeholder strings not using t() in admin forms
description: Approximately 183 input placeholder attributes use hardcoded strings (e.g., placeholder="Search...", placeholder="Nom du projet") instead of t() calls. Found in 49+ files, predominantly in admin/comptabilite/, admin/telephonie/, admin/crm/, and admin/platform/ sections. This violates the project's i18n rule that ALL user-facing text must use t().
fix: Replace each hardcoded placeholder with placeholder={t('namespace.placeholderKey')}. Add corresponding keys to all 22 locale files.

title: Entire telephonie section (16 pages) lacks i18n
description: All 16 pages in the admin/telephonie/ section have no i18n integration. These are wrapper pages delegating to *Client.tsx components, but the client components (ParametresClient, GroupesClient, CampagnesClient, etc.) also use hardcoded French strings. This is the largest section without i18n coverage.
fix: Add i18n to all telephonie client components. Create admin.telephonie.* translation keys in all 22 locale files.

title: Platform management and subscription pages lack i18n
description: The platform management pages (platform/page.tsx, platform/tenants/[id], platform/assisted-setup) and subscription management pages (abonnement/plan, abonnement/domaine, abonnement/modules, abonnement/page) all use hardcoded French strings without i18n. These are used by super-admins and tenants managing their subscriptions.
fix: Add useI18n() to each page and replace hardcoded strings with t() calls.

title: Minimal tabIndex usage across interactive elements
description: Only 13 files in the entire codebase use tabIndex, with a total of 16 occurrences. Given 736 files with onClick handlers and 38 non-button interactive elements, keyboard navigation coverage is very low. Custom interactive widgets (kanban boards, media galleries, inline editors, softphone controls) likely cannot be reached via Tab key navigation.
fix: Audit all custom interactive components. Add tabIndex={0} to focusable non-button interactive elements. For composite widgets (kanban, gallery), implement arrow key navigation pattern per WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices.

file: 4 files with native tags in non-email contexts
title: Native tags used instead of Next.js Image component (4 files)
description: 4 files use native HTML tags: (1) account/settings/page.tsx:705 - MFA QR code (data URL, acceptable), (2) TemplateBuilder.tsx:43 - email HTML template (acceptable), (3) tutoriels/page.tsx:311 - markdown parser output (acceptable in HTML string), (4) mobile/receipt-capture/page.tsx:88 - receipt preview (data URL, acceptable). All have alt attributes. The codebase generally uses next/image correctly (88 files import from 'next/image').
fix: No critical action needed. The 4 uses of native are justified (data URLs and HTML string generation). Continue using next/image for all other cases.

file: 96 files with alt= out of 88 using next/image
title: Image alt attributes present but quality varies
description: 96 files have alt= attributes, covering 137 occurrences. All next/image usages (88 files) include alt props, which is good. However, many alt texts are generic or non-descriptive: 'MFA QR Code', 'Logo', empty strings for decorative images. Some dynamic images (product images, user avatars, media library) may have empty or repetitive alt texts depending on database content. No systematic pattern for decorative images (alt='') vs informative images.
fix: Audit alt text quality. For product images, use product name as alt. For decorative images, use alt='' explicitly. For user avatars, use username. Add alt text guidelines to component documentation.

title: 130+ inline hardcoded color values may cause contrast issues
description: Found 130 instances of inline style={{ color: '#...' }} across 26 files. Concentrated in: legal pages (cookies-en.tsx:11, conditions:6, confidentialite:5), contact/page.tsx:27, a-propos/page.tsx:13, BlogComments.tsx:6, account/invoices:16. These hardcoded colors bypass the design system tokens (ios26-tokens.css) and may not maintain proper contrast ratios in both light and dark themes. The project uses Tailwind CSS with dark mode support, but inline styles don't respect dark mode.
fix: Replace inline color styles with Tailwind CSS classes that support dark mode: text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 instead of style={{ color: '#334155' }}. For brand colors, use CSS custom properties from ios26-tokens.css.

title: Loading and error boundaries well-covered (339 loading, 126 error files)
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The codebase has excellent coverage of Next.js loading states (339 loading.tsx files for 354 pages) and error boundaries (126 error.tsx files). Additionally, 45 pages use inline loading patterns (isLoading, Skeleton, Suspense). There is also a global-error.tsx at the app root.
fix: No critical action needed. Consider adding error.tsx files for the remaining ~228 routes that don't have them (mostly admin pages that share parent error boundaries).

title: 113 pages may lack responsive design (no sm:/md:/lg:/xl: breakpoints)
description: 113 of 354 page.tsx files contain no responsive Tailwind breakpoint classes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:), flex-col, grid-cols, or hidden utility classes. Many are thin server component wrappers that delegate rendering to client components which DO have responsive design. However, at least 30-40 pages are actual client components with layout content but no responsive breakpoints. These pages may not adapt properly to mobile screens.
fix: Audit the 113 pages, filtering out thin wrappers. For pages with actual content: (1) Add responsive padding (px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8), (2) Use responsive grid (grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3), (3) Stack columns on mobile (flex-col sm:flex-row). Focus on platform/page.tsx (grid-cols-4 without sm: variant).

title: 68% of pages are client components - many could be server components
description: 242 of 354 page.tsx files use 'use client' directive, while only 112 are server components. Many admin pages are large client components (20+ over 1000 lines) that could benefit from a server/client split pattern: server component for data fetching, client component for interactivity. The largest client component pages are 2425, 2328, 1986, 1835, 1815 lines. This increases JavaScript bundle size and prevents server-side rendering optimizations.
fix: For large client component pages (>500 lines): (1) Extract data fetching into a server component wrapper, (2) Pass data as props to a smaller client component, (3) Use React Server Components for static parts of the page. Priority: public pages first (SEO benefit), then frequently accessed admin pages.

title: Contact page has 27 inline color styles - dark mode incompatible
description: The public contact page has 27 inline style={{ color: '#...' }} declarations. These hardcoded colors will not adapt to dark mode, causing potential readability issues (dark text on dark background or vice versa) when users toggle the theme. This is a customer-facing page.
fix: Replace all inline color styles with Tailwind classes that support dark mode variants. Example: style={{ color: '#1e293b' }} becomes className="text-slate-800 dark:text-slate-200".

title: About page has 13 inline color styles - dark mode incompatible
description: The public about page has 13 inline style={{ color: '#...' }} declarations that bypass the dark mode system. Customer-facing page with potential contrast issues in dark mode.
fix: Replace inline colors with Tailwind classes supporting dark mode.

title: Invoices page has 16 inline color styles for invoice rendering
description: The account invoices page has 16 inline style={{ color: '#...' }} declarations. These are likely used for invoice styling/print, but they bypass dark mode support and may cause contrast issues when viewing invoices in dark mode.
fix: For printable content, consider using a print-specific CSS class. For screen display, use Tailwind dark mode classes.

title: Cookies legal page has 11 inline color styles
description: The cookies policy page has 11 inline color styles that bypass the design system and dark mode. Legal pages should be readable in all themes for compliance.
fix: Replace inline colors with Tailwind dark-mode-aware classes.

fix: Add useI18n() and replace all strings with t() calls. High priority due to conversion impact.

title: Good overall ARIA adoption with 609 files using aria-* attributes

Good coverage in: shop components (Header, CartDrawer, SearchModal, ProductCard), form components (AddressAutocomplete, LanguageSelector), voip components (Softphone, IncomingCallModal). Error pages consistently use aria-label for retry buttons.

Key pages: contact, demo, aide (help), tarifs (pricing), securite, a-propos and sub-pages (valeurs, engagements, equipe, histoire, mission), mentions-legales (3 pages), accessibilite, actualites, clients and sub-pages. These are important for SEO and social sharing.
fix: Add metadata to all public pages. Use generateMetadata() for dynamic locale-aware titles. Include og:image pointing to /api/og route.

file: Multiple admin pages with error silently swallowed
title: Error handling pattern: catch blocks with silent failures
description: Several admin pages follow a pattern of catch {} or catch { // Error handled silently } in fetch calls. Examples: platform/page.tsx:45-46, platform/assisted-setup. When API calls fail, users see no feedback - the page simply doesn't load data or shows stale state. This is poor UX as users don't know if something went wrong.
fix: Add user-visible error feedback in catch blocks: use toast.error() or set an error state that renders an error message. Minimum: toast.error(t('common.error.fetchFailed')).

title: Shop header and footer have good ARIA implementation
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The shop Header.tsx uses aria-label extensively (16 occurrences), has proper role attributes, and implements keyboard-accessible navigation. The Footer has aria-label for newsletter input and social links. This is a good reference implementation for other components.
fix: No action needed. Use as reference for fixing other components.

title: Admin forms lack client-side validation feedback patterns
description: Many admin forms use disabled submit buttons (disabled={!field1 || !field2}) but don't explain WHY the button is disabled. Users with cognitive disabilities may not understand which field is missing. The pattern in platform/assisted-setup is: disabled={!client.name || !client.email || !client.slug} with no per-field validation messages.
fix: Add per-field validation with visible error messages. Use aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby pointing to the error message. Show inline errors when fields are empty after the user has interacted with them.

title: Good i18n coverage overall: 248 of 354 pages (70%) use i18n
description: POSITIVE FINDING: 248 pages import and use i18n hooks (useI18n or useTranslation) or server i18n functions. All 22 locale files exist with translations. The i18n system supports client-side (useI18n, useTranslation) and server-side (getServerLocale, createServerTranslator) patterns. Language selector, locale detection, and RTL support (dir attribute) are implemented.
fix: Focus on the remaining 106 pages that lack i18n coverage, prioritizing customer-facing pages.

title: Security audits page lacks i18n
description: The security audits admin page has no i18n integration.
fix: Add i18n when next modified.

title: Shared DataTable component has minimal a11y support
description: The DataTable component (used across many admin pages) has only 3 aria-label occurrences, 2 focus: styles, 1 tabIndex, and 1 clickable <tr>. As the primary data display component in the admin area, it needs better a11y: sortable column headers need aria-sort, clickable rows need keyboard support, pagination needs proper labeling.
fix: Add: (1) aria-sort on sortable column headers, (2) role='button' and keyboard handlers on clickable rows, (3) aria-label on pagination controls, (4) Announce sort changes with aria-live region.

title: Public documentation page lacks i18n
description: The public documentation/API docs page has no i18n integration. Documentation should be available in the user's preferred language.
fix: Add i18n support to the documentation page.

title: SearchModal has 4 aria-labels - good search accessibility

fix: No action needed.

file: Multiple form pages across admin section
title: Forms use disabled buttons without explaining why action is unavailable
description: A common pattern across admin forms: submit buttons have disabled={!field1 || !field2} conditions. When disabled, the button shows reduced opacity (opacity-50) but no tooltip or message explaining what's missing. This is particularly challenging for users who rely on screen readers, as they encounter a disabled button with no context.
fix: Add aria-disabled instead of disabled, with aria-describedby pointing to helper text that explains what is needed. Or show inline validation messages on empty required fields.

title: VoIP Incoming Call Modal has 5 aria attributes and 3 aria-labels
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The IncomingCallModal has 5 ARIA attributes including aria-live for real-time announcements and proper aria-labels on answer/decline buttons. Good accessibility for an urgent notification component.
fix: No action needed.

file: 185 files have focus: styles out of 736 with onClick
title: Only 25% of interactive files have visible focus styles
description: 185 files out of 736 files with onClick handlers have focus: CSS classes (25%). This means 75% of interactive components may lack visible focus indicators. WCAG 2.4.7 requires a visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation. While many interactive elements are <button> (which has browser default focus), custom interactive elements and styled buttons with outline-none may lose focus visibility.
fix: Audit interactive components for focus visibility. Add focus:ring-2 focus:ring-offset-2 focus:ring-blue-500 (or equivalent) to all clickable elements. Consider a global CSS rule for focus-visible.

file: admin/abonnement/* (4 pages: plan, domaine, modules, main)

title: RecordingPlayer has 11 aria-labels and 2 tabIndex - good media player a11y
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The call recording player has extensive accessibility support with 11 aria-labels (play, pause, volume, speed, seek), 2 tabIndex for keyboard navigation. Good reference for media player accessibility.

title: AddressAutocomplete has 14 aria attributes - best practice autocomplete

title: Admin command palette has aria-label and keyboard support
description: POSITIVE FINDING: The AdminCommandPalette (Cmd+K shortcut) has aria-label on the search input and keyboard navigation support. Important for admin power users who rely on keyboard shortcuts.
fix: Ensure results list items have proper roles and are keyboard navigable (role='listbox' and role='option').

file: Only 7 occurrences of focus-visible: across 5 files
title: Almost no use of focus-visible: for keyboard-only focus styles
description: The focus-visible: pseudo-class (which shows focus only for keyboard users, not mouse clicks) is used in only 5 files (7 occurrences): VideoPlayer.tsx, ContentList.tsx, admin/Button.tsx, bannieres/page.tsx, abonnements/page.tsx. The codebase uses 756 focus: occurrences but only 7 focus-visible: ones. Modern best practice is focus-visible: for non-mouse focus indication.
fix: Adopt focus-visible: as the default focus styling approach. Add a Tailwind utility class or custom CSS rule: *:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring-color); outline-offset: 2px; }. Gradually replace focus: with focus-visible: across components.

file: 4 files with arbitrary Tailwind text colors: text-[#xxx]

fix: Replace text-[#xxx] with design system Tailwind classes (text-blue-600, text-slate-700, etc.) or CSS custom properties. Verify contrast ratio meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).

title: 49 arbitrary Tailwind background colors bypass design system
description: 27 files use bg-[#hexcolor] arbitrary Tailwind values (49 occurrences). Concentrated in: platform pages (11), account/referrals (9), various shop pages (9), and scattered across other pages. These bypass the design system and may cause visual inconsistency.
fix: Replace with design system Tailwind classes. Use bg-blue-600, bg-slate-100, etc. For brand colors, define them in tailwind.config.ts as custom colors.

title: Checkout page well-accessibilized with 22 focus:ring, 6 aria-labels, DOMPurify

It uses i18n via useTranslation. This is the best-implemented page from an a11y perspective.

title: Quick entry page has safe arithmetic parser replacing eval()

title: console.log statements absent but console.error/warn used directly in 15 files
description: While zero console.log() calls were found, there are 15+ files using console.error() or console.warn() directly instead of the structured logger. Files: auditors/base-auditor.ts:42, admin/admin-fetch.ts:77, auditors/i18n-completeness.ts:55, auditors/azure-local-sync.ts:342/382/399/431, auditors/typescript-quality.ts:175, fetch-with-retry.ts:29, accounting/types.ts:146, accounting/pwa.service.ts:88/129/158.
